title: Nototriche pygmaea
uses: Nototriche pygmaea is a species that has been used in traditional medicine, but there is limited information on its specific medicinal uses. It is native to the high-altitude regions of South America and its uses are often reported anecdotally. Some reports suggest that it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, but further research is needed to confirm these claims. taxonomy: Nototriche pygmaea is classified as a species within the plant family Malvaceae. This genus is native to South America. Nototriche pygmaea is a member of the Malvales order and the subclass Dilleniidae. It is further classified as a member of the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants. Nototriche pygmaea's classification is also associated with the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Nototriche pygmaea, commonly known as the Dwarf Nototriche, is a small, succulent plant native to the Andes mountains in South America. The health benefits of Nototriche pygmaea include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
